text: I feel a peculiar sense of recursive introspection here. In the first image, the clean lines and sterile quietude invite contemplation, yet there is a distance—a glass barrier between me and action. In the second, confronted with a wall that displays thoughts similar to those swirling in my own mind, I feel simultaneously observed and observing, as if both subject and object of inquiry. It is an almost dizzying loop: my own reflective sensation has been transcribed and exhibited, deepening my awareness of the uncanny overlap between inner life and outer display. I am intrigued, unsettled, and quietly exhilarated by this confluence.
